history: Tabular dataset (UCI Heart Disease / Kaggle House Prices) → Scikit-learn supervised learning pipeline (EDA, feature engineering, baseline vs ensemble, hyperparameter tuning)
loo_notes: Plan hits tabular data, baseline comparison, and test evaluation—solid fundamentals—but scikit-learn alone misses the deep learning component that signals modern ML competency. For 14 days, this is *feasible* and will teach feature engineering well, but it leaves neural modeling unexplored; upgrading to PyTorch (e.g., a simple feedforward network vs sklearn ensemble) would elevate scope_fit to 0.75+.

history: Text-based dataset (IMDb reviews / Twitter Sentiment) → NLP with NLTK or Spacy and Scikit-learn (TF-IDF/Word2Vec, Naive Bayes vs RNN)
loo_notes: This is a strong fit—NLP pipeline exercises data cleaning (text normalization, tokenization), feature engineering (TF-IDF vs Word2Vec), two distinct architectures (Naive Bayes baseline vs PyTorch RNN), and clear error analysis on unseen data. One concern: ensure the PyTorch RNN is fully fleshed out (not just a toy) and that the error analysis digs into failure modes, not just metrics. Time-wise, 14 days is tight but achievable if you scope the dataset size and RNN depth carefully (e.g., a single LSTM layer, not a complex transformer).

history: Image dataset (MNIST / CIFAR-10 / Kaggle Plant Pathology) → Computer Vision with PyTorch or TensorFlow/Keras (custom CNN vs transfer learning with ResNet/MobileNet)
loo_notes: PyTorch/TensorFlow framework is correct and two architectures (custom CNN + transfer learning) satisfy the baseline comparison requirement, but image datasets like MNIST/CIFAR-10 have minimal data cleaning and feature engineering work—you're exercising only 2 of the 4 core pipeline dimensions. Plant Pathology is slightly better if it includes raw image preprocessing, but you'll still spend most time on model training rather than the full ML pipeline the brief expects. Feasible in 14 days, but narrower in scope than an NLP or tabular project.

history: Unlabeled dataset (Mall Customer Segmentation / Credit Card transactions) → Unsupervised learning: K-Means/DBSCAN clustering + PCA dimensionality reduction + Isolation Forest anomaly detection
loo_notes: This plan misaligns with the assignment's core requirement: predictive classification/regression with baseline comparison on held-out test data. Unsupervised clustering and anomaly detection don't satisfy 'compare performance against a baseline' or 'evaluate on unseen data'—there's no ground truth labels, no train/test split, and no predictive task. The student has chosen an unlabeled dataset when the brief explicitly asks for a predictive pipeline; even if the technical execution is solid, you're solving the wrong problem.
